title: Why Your BMI Calculator Is Probably Lying to You
excerpt: I've seen patients celebrate 'normal' BMIs while their metabolic panels told a completely different story. Here's what most calculators get wrong.

title: What 15 Years of Metabolic Data Actually Looks Like
excerpt: After reviewing over 12,000 patient records, one pattern is clear: BMI tells you almost nothing about metabolic health without context.

title: The Asian BMI Threshold: Why American Doctors Get It Wrong
excerpt: My mother has a BMI of 23.4. In American medicine, that's 'normal.' In reality, she's pre-diabetic with elevated triglycerides. Here's why.

title: Pediatric BMI: Why Adult Calculators Harm Children
excerpt: A parent recently told me their 8-year-old was 'obese' because an adult BMI calculator said so. The child was in the 85th percentile — overweight, not obese.

title: BMI vs Body Fat Percentage: The Real Debate
excerpt: Body fat percentage is more accurate than BMI. But it's also harder to measure, more expensive, and not always more clinically useful.

title: The BMI Rebound: Why Lost Weight Returns
excerpt: I've watched hundreds of patients lose 20, 30, 50 pounds. And I've watched most of them gain it back. The metabolic adaptation is real.

title: Waist Circumference: The Missing Piece
excerpt: I've had patients with 'normal' BMIs but 40-inch waists. Their metabolic panels looked like disaster zones. Here's why waist measurement is non-negotiable.

title: Why I Stopped Telling Patients Their 'Ideal Weight'
excerpt: For years, I gave patients a number. 'Your ideal weight is 165 pounds.' Then I watched them obsess, fail, and feel like failures.

title: Metabolic Health: Beyond the Scale
excerpt: I have patients who weigh 200 pounds and are metabolically healthier than patients who weigh 150. Here's what actually determines metabolic health.

title: The NIH Study That Changed How I See BMI
excerpt: During my time at NIH NIDDK, I worked on a cohort study that followed 5,000 patients for 10 years. The findings challenged everything I learned in medical school.

title: BMI Calculators for Athletes: Why They Fail
excerpt: A college football player came to my clinic with a BMI of 32. His coach was panicking about 'obesity.' His body fat was 8%.

title: How I Use BMI in My Clinic: The Real Protocol
excerpt: After 15 years, I've developed a specific protocol for using BMI without falling into its traps. Here's exactly what I do during a new patient visit.

title: The Future of BMI: What Comes Next
excerpt: BMI is a 200-year-old tool. In 10 years, we'll look back and wonder why we ever used it. Here's what's replacing it.
